subjects: bash-lite: fix the sandbox never becoming cross-origin isolated
Add experimental in-browser Linux execution sandbox + bash-lite agent

days.summary: Shipped the experimental in-browser Linux execution sandbox and its bash-lite agent, and added client-side RAG for Se/cure conversations and projects (browser-direct OpenAI embeddings, sealed in local state). The ghost button was repurposed as the door to the /cure client tier. Plus two-font URL branding and iOS/ghost-navigation fixes.

days.summary: Extended the sandbox to mount chat and project files into the Linux VM, gave both tiers a unified settings gear with a one-field API-key form (provider auto-detect), and brought Berget in as a third client-side Se/cure provider. A large day of sandbox research and design docs, plus the anonymous-visitor /cure fix (splitting vault-core out of the public graph) and COEP/iOS cross-origin-isolation fixes.

days.summary: Introspection mode (the developer_mode knob) landed on both tiers: a conversation can ask about the site’s own deployed source, answered from a committed source-RAG snapshot, with the TIN mascot and a private-vs-remote model picker. Added the /architecture/ story page and first-visit onboarding animations, the admin security-risk review board and a unified boards discovery index, plus a round of security hardening (secret-leak scanner, in-flight concurrency caps, bounded outbound fetches) and a clarity refactor of the largest orchestrators.
